input:
- # Tine ⟷ OG parity test graph

A shared Logseq graph (standard `pages/` + `journals/` layout) for verifying that
Tine matches OG (original Logseq). **Open the same folder in both apps and compare.**

Convention: **every Tine feature or bug fix adds a small, self-contained example
here** — one page (or a few) that exercises the behavior, so we can eyeball it
side by side in OG and Tine and catch parity gaps. Keep examples minimal and
labeled by the feature they test.

**Reference screenshots live in `assets/`** so they survive across sessions:
`<feature>-og.png` is the oracle (Martin screenshots real OG), `<feature>-tine.png`
is what Claude verified against it. When a feature lands, Claude drops its Tine
shot in and notes exactly which page(s) to screenshot in OG.

Markdown format; `:file/name-format :triple-lowbar` (so `a/b` ⇒ `a___b.md`).

## Examples

| Feature | Pages | What to check | Shots |
|---------|-------|---------------|-------|
| Namespaces (macro + Hierarchy) | `Formula1`, `Formula1/2026`, `Formula1/2026/08 Austrian Grand Prix`, `Formula1/2026/09 Italian Grand Prix`, `Formula1/2025/12 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix` (note: **no `Formula1/2025` page of its own**) | Open **Formula1**: `{{namespace Formula1}}` shows a bold **Namespace** label + the bulleted descendant tree. The automatic **Hierarchy** section lists **one bulleted row per namespace level** as a breadcrumb (`[[Formula1]] / [[2026]] / …`) — including a synthesized **`[[Formula1]] / [[2025]]`** row even though that page has no file. `icon::` is hidden (shown as the title icon, not a property row). | `assets/namespace-tine.png` (Tine); add `assets/namespace-og.png` (real OG) |
| Macros (easy + medium tier) | `Macros` (+ `:macros` in `logseq/config.edn`) | Open **Macros**: `{{twitter}}`=`{{tweet}}`, `{{vimeo id}}`/`{{bilibili BV…}}` iframe embeds, `{{img url w h}}`/`{{img url center}}` sizing+alignment, and **user `:macros`** — `{{poem red, blue}}`→"Roses are red, violets are blue." and `{{hi Martin, Macros}}`→bold + `[[Macros]]` link. `{{youtube-timestamp}}`/`{{cloze}}`/`{{zotero-*}}` render **degraded** (Tine has no YT-player API / SRS / Zotero connector) and say so — expected to differ from OG. | `screenshots/macros.png` (Tine, in repo); add `assets/macros-og.png` (real OG) |

* Boundary-safe plain text: a/b/c file paths, snake_case_var, and 2*3*4 all stay literal (no accidental emphasis)

input: * A namespace is purely name-based: a page named =a/b/c= is "under" =a/b=. There is no special property — only the =/= in the page name. On disk the =/= is encoded (see the filenames: =mwe-namespace___alpha.org= uses triple-underscore for =/=).

inline.text: A namespace is purely name-based: a page named
. There is no special property — only the
in the page name. On disk the

input: * Try it: open [[mwe-namespace/alpha/deep]] and look at the breadcrumb above its title.

inline.text: and look at the breadcrumb above its title.

input: * This is =mwe-namespace/alpha=, a child of [[mwe-namespace]]. (Claude-created example.)
* Above the title you should see a breadcrumb: =mwe-namespace= (clickable, jumps to the parent).

inline.text: Above the title you should see a breadcrumb:

input: * It has its own child one level deeper: [[mwe-namespace/alpha/deep]].

inline.text: It has its own child one level deeper:

input: * This is =mwe-namespace/alpha/deep=, two levels under [[mwe-namespace]]. (Claude-created example.)
* Its breadcrumb should read =mwe-namespace › alpha= (both clickable ancestors).
* This is =mwe-namespace/beta=, a sibling of [[mwe-namespace/alpha]] and a child of [[mwe-namespace]]. (Claude-created example.)
* Unlike alpha, beta has no deeper children — so its "Namespace" section is empty.

inline.text: Unlike alpha, beta has no deeper children — so its "Namespace" section is empty.
